title: EU Fines Google Over Competition Rules
subtitle: The European Commission orders Google to pay nearly €900 million for breaking digital market laws.

summary.en: The European Union has fined Google €890 million for violating competition laws. The company was punished for favoring its own services in search results and limiting how app developers can offer cheaper prices. This decision follows similar fines against Apple and Meta under the Digital Markets Act. Google has criticized the ruling, claiming it will hurt European consumers and businesses. The move comes at a tense time for EU-US trade relations.
summary.zh: 歐盟因 Google 違反競爭法而對其處以八億九千萬歐元的罰款。該公司因在搜尋結果中偏袒自家服務，並限制應用程式開發者提供更優惠價格而受罰。此項決定繼蘋果與 Meta 依據《數位市場法》受罰後做出。Google 批評此裁決，聲稱這將損害歐洲消費者與企業的利益。此舉正值歐盟與美國貿易關係緊張之際。

paragraphs.en: The European Commission has ordered Google to pay €890 million (about $1 billion) in fines for breaking European Union competition laws. This is a major development in the EU’s ongoing effort to regulate large technology companies. The Commission, which acts as the executive branch of the EU, stated that Google used its search engine and app store to unfairly favor its own products over those of its competitors.
paragraphs.zh: 歐盟執行委員會（European Commission）已下令 Google 支付八億九千萬歐元（約合十億美元）的罰款，理由是該公司違反了歐盟的競爭法。這是歐盟持續監管大型科技公司過程中的一項重大進展。作為歐盟行政部門的執行委員會指出，Google 利用其搜尋引擎與應用程式商店，以不公平的方式優化自家產品，使其在競爭中佔據優勢。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Technology, the fine is split into two parts. Google must pay €460 million because it gave priority to its own services, such as hotel and shopping deals, in search results. Another €430 million fine was issued because Google prevented app developers from telling customers about cheaper subscription offers on other websites or alternative app stores. Ars Technica reports that these practices have gone unchecked for a long time, leading the EU to take this firm action under the Digital Markets Act (DMA).
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報科技版》（The Guardian Technology）報導，這筆罰款分為兩部分。Google 必須支付四億六千萬歐元，因為該公司在搜尋結果中優先顯示自家服務，例如飯店與購物優惠。另一筆四億三千萬歐元的罰款則是因為 Google 限制應用程式開發者，禁止他們告知客戶在其他網站或替代應用程式商店能以更便宜的價格訂閱服務。科技媒體《Ars Technica》報導稱，這些行為長期以來未受約束，促使歐盟依據《數位市場法》（Digital Markets Act，簡稱 DMA）採取此項強硬措施。

paragraphs.en: The Digital Markets Act is a set of rules designed to ensure that large tech companies, often called "gatekeepers," do not abuse their power. The goal is to create a fair environment where smaller companies can compete. Under the new ruling, Google must now treat third-party services in its search results in a fair and non-discriminatory way. Additionally, the company must allow app developers to promote offers outside of the Google Play store.
paragraphs.zh: 《數位市場法》是一套旨在確保大型科技公司（通常被稱為「守門人」）不會濫用其權力的規則。其目標是創造一個公平的環境，讓規模較小的公司能夠參與競爭。根據這項新裁決，Google 現在必須以公平且無差別的方式處理搜尋結果中的第三方服務。此外，該公司必須允許應用程式開發者在 Google Play 商店之外推廣優惠活動。

paragraphs.en: This is not the first time the EU has used the DMA to punish big tech. Last year, Apple was fined €500 million for similar issues in its app store, and Meta was fined €200 million regarding its advertising practices on Facebook and Instagram. Max von Thun, a director at the Open Markets Institute Europe, told The Guardian Technology that while these fines are a step forward, they are the "bare minimum" for a company that earned over $400 billion in revenue last year. He urged the commission to move quickly to ensure Google stops these anti-competitive practices permanently.
paragraphs.zh: 這並非歐盟首次利用《數位市場法》懲罰大型科技公司。去年，蘋果（Apple）因應用程式商店的類似問題被罰款五億歐元，Meta 也因其在 Facebook 與 Instagram 上的廣告行為被罰款兩億歐元。「開放市場研究所歐洲分部」（Open Markets Institute Europe）總監馬克斯·馮·圖恩（Max von Thun）向《衛報科技版》表示，雖然這些罰款是向前邁出的一步，但對於去年營收超過四千億美元的公司來說，這僅是「最低限度」的懲罰。他敦促委員會迅速採取行動，確保 Google 永久停止這些反競爭行為。

paragraphs.en: Google has strongly disagreed with the decision. Kent Walker, the company’s president of global affairs, described the fine as a result of complaints from a small group of companies that only care about their own interests. He argued that the DMA forces Google to remove useful features that European users enjoy, such as instant pricing for flights and hotels. Google has the right to appeal the decision and may ask for a suspension of the fines while the case is reviewed.
paragraphs.zh: Google 對此項決定表示強烈反對。該公司全球事務總裁肯特·沃克（Kent Walker）形容這筆罰款是一小群只關心自身利益的公司所提出的投訴結果。他主張，《數位市場法》迫使 Google 移除歐洲用戶喜愛的實用功能，例如航班與飯店的即時價格顯示。Google 有權對此決定提出上訴，並可能在案件審理期間要求暫緩執行罰款。

paragraphs.en: The timing of this fine has created political tension. The announcement comes just as temporary global tariffs—taxes on imported goods—are set to expire. According to Ars Technica, 25 Republican lawmakers in the United States have written to Donald Trump, asking him to retaliate against the EU. These lawmakers are concerned that the EU is using the DMA to unfairly target American companies. Some critics also argue that the rules are unfair because popular Chinese companies, such as Temu and AliExpress, are not held to the same strict standards.
paragraphs.zh: 這筆罰款的時間點引發了政治緊張。此公告發布之際，正值全球臨時關稅（針對進口商品徵收的稅款）即將到期。據《Ars Technica》報導，美國有二十五位共和黨籍國會議員已致函川普（Donald Trump），要求他對歐盟採取報復措施。這些議員擔心歐盟正利用《數位市場法》不公平地針對美國企業。部分批評者也認為這些規則不公平，因為熱門的中國企業，如 Temu 與全球速賣通（AliExpress），並未受到同樣嚴格的標準限制。

paragraphs.en: Despite these concerns, a senior official for the EU insisted that the bloc has the "sovereign right" to regulate tech companies operating within its borders. The official stated that the timing of the fine was not connected to trade tariffs or the reactions of the US government. They emphasized that European consumers will benefit from these changes, as they will have more choices and potentially lower prices when searching for services online.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管存在這些擔憂，歐盟的一位資深官員堅稱，歐盟擁有監管境內科技公司的「主權權利」。該官員表示，罰款的時間點與貿易關稅或美國政府的反應無關。他們強調，歐洲消費者將從這些變革中受益，因為他們在線上搜尋服務時將擁有更多選擇，且價格可能更低。

paragraphs.en: Google has 60 days to make the required changes to its services. If the company fails to comply, it could face additional daily fines. The European Commission noted that Google has already begun testing changes to how it displays search results, which the commission described as "substantial progress." As the situation develops, the focus will remain on whether these changes truly open the market to more competition or if they will lead to a long legal battle between the tech giant and European regulators.
paragraphs.zh: Google 有六十天的時間對其服務進行必要的調整。若該公司未能遵守，可能面臨額外的每日罰款。歐盟執行委員會指出，Google 已經開始測試搜尋結果顯示方式的變更，委員會將此描述為「實質進展」。隨著事態發展，焦點將持續放在這些變更是否真的能為市場帶來更多競爭，或者是否會導致這家科技巨頭與歐洲監管機構之間爆發長期的法律戰。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A division or separation of something into parts.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（分開），這裡作名詞用，指罰款被分成兩部分。文中：According to The Guardian Technology, the fine is split into two parts.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Not controlled or restrained; allowed to continue without interference.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 未受抑制的、任其發展的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞在文中描述行為未被阻止，容易誤解為「未檢查」。文中：Ars Technica reports that these practices have gone unchecked for a long time, leading the EU to take this firm action under the Digital Markets Act (DMA).

keyTerms.definitionEn: A group of countries or organizations that work together for a common purpose.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞常指政治或經濟聯盟，容易與「積木」(block) 拼寫混淆。文中：Despite these concerns, a senior official for the EU insisted that the bloc has the "sovereign right" to regulate tech companies operating within its borders.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: EU fines Google €890m for competition breaches over search and apps

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Google hit with $1 billion in fines as EU braces for Trump battle

previousArticles.title: The Future of Truth: Detection and Regulation
previousArticles.summaryEn: This article examines the ongoing battle between AI-generated deepfakes and detection technology. It also discusses the challenges of implementing international regulations like the EU AI Act. Finally, it explores the limitations of watermarking as a solution for verifying authentic content.

quiz.question: What is the specific reason Google was fined €430 million?

options.A: For favoring its own hotel and shopping deals in search results.
options.B: For restricting app developers from informing users about cheaper options elsewhere.
options.C: For violating the Digital Markets Act by failing to pay global import tariffs.
options.D: For charging excessive fees to users who purchase items through the Google Play store.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，這筆 4.3 億歐元的罰款是因為 Google 阻止應用程式開發者告知客戶在其他網站或替代應用程式商店有更便宜的訂閱優惠。選項 A 是另一筆 4.6 億歐元罰款的原因（優先顯示自家服務）。選項 C 與罰款無關，關稅問題是在文章後段提到的政治緊張因素。選項 D 雖然與應用程式商店有關，但文章並未提到是因為「收費過高」而罰款，而是因為「資訊限制」。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA) and American tech companies?

options.A: The DMA is primarily designed to prevent Chinese companies from entering the European market.
options.B: American tech companies are fully supportive of the DMA because it creates a more stable business environment.
options.C: The EU believes it has the legal authority to regulate any tech company that does business within its borders, regardless of its country of origin.
options.D: The US government has officially agreed to stop criticizing the EU's regulations to avoid a trade war.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第七段，歐盟官員強調歐盟擁有「主權權利」（sovereign right）來監管在其境內營運的科技公司。這暗示了無論公司總部在哪裡（如美國），只要在歐盟境內營運就受其管轄。選項 A 錯誤，文章提到中國公司未受同樣嚴格標準規範。選項 B 錯誤，Google 高層明確表示反對。選項 D 錯誤，文章指出美國議員甚至威脅要進行報復，並未達成協議。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the phrase 'bare minimum' imply regarding the fines imposed on Google?

options.A: The fines are insufficient to truly change the company's behavior.
options.B: The fines are the lowest amount allowed by European law.
options.C: The fines are a temporary measure that will soon be removed.
options.D: The fines are the only way the EU can generate revenue from tech giants.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第四段中，Max von Thun 提到這些罰款相比於 Google 去年超過 4000 億美元的營收來說只是「bare minimum」（最低限度），意指這些處罰金額對 Google 來說太小，不足以產生實質的威懾力。選項 B 錯誤，文中未提及這是法律規定的最低金額。選項 C 錯誤，這並非暫時措施。選項 D 錯誤，罰款並非歐盟的主要收入來源，其目的是為了監管市場競爭。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of this article?

options.A: The history of the Digital Markets Act and its impact on global trade tariffs.
options.B: The legal battle between Google and the US government over trade policies.
options.C: The European Commission's efforts to regulate big tech companies through recent fines and new rules.
options.D: The success of Chinese tech companies in bypassing European competition laws.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。整篇文章的核心在於報導歐盟委員會依據《數位市場法》（DMA）對 Google 開罰，並討論此舉對科技市場競爭的影響、各方反應及未來展望。選項 A 過於狹隘，關稅僅是文中提到的一個背景因素。選項 B 錯誤，這主要是歐盟與 Google 的糾紛，而非美國政府與 Google。選項 D 錯誤，文章僅在一段中提到中國公司未受同樣嚴格規範，並非文章主旨。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
